content.text: Pygame-dom uses states to make the UI reactive. You can use states to change element's text or to change some of the attributes like inline style.
Example below shows how to create a simple state:

code: score: UIState = UIState("score", 0)

content.text: States are written into {}. You can use simple python operations, some of the python's built in functions like len() or even a simple one-line condition. State expressions in {} have access to all created states and can call user made functions that are registered.
You can get and set the value of the state using the state's value property:
